steps.title: Track the passage's logic
steps.content: Asiedu finds a negative correlation: as natural-resource extraction grows as a share of a developing country's economy, foreign investment falls. The passage calls this "counterintuitive" (since extraction needs large initial investment) and then explains the cause—boom-bust cycles, currency instability, and vulnerability to shocks create uncertainty that investors avoid.

steps.title: Define what the main idea must contain
steps.content: The right answer must include (1) the surprise/expectation reversal and (2) the actual cause—instability/uncertainty driving investors away. Anything that misnames the cause or reverses the direction fails.

steps.title: Eliminate by misread/overreach
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'decline happens because... initial investments too large for foreign investors to supply': misnames the cause—the passage says scale is exactly what multinationals CAN meet; the actual cause is instability, not investor inability. ✗
  • Choice B — 'less dependent on foreign investment': misframes the finding as countries choosing independence; the passage describes investors choosing to withdraw due to risk. ✗
  • Choice C — 'one might expect... investment would increase... the opposite happens because heavy reliance... can lead to unattractive conditions for investors': captures both the expectation reversal and the correct cause (unattractive/unstable conditions). ✓
  • Choice D — 'foreign investment may increase significantly as those industries stabilize': reverses the direction—the passage explains why investment falls, not how it might rise. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
